column1: # Typed function-parameter dispatch.
# `fnq [ params ] [ returns ] [ body ]` builds a function definition
# value. Param tokens have the form `name:TypeName` — the spec
# tokenizer is whitespace-only, so each param arrives as a single
# Word and the `fnq` handler splits on `:` to recover the
# (name, type) pairq. The body references each param by name; on
# invocation, the param values are pushed onto the defq stack so the
# body's bare-name references resolve to them.
# Pair this with `defq`: `defq NAME ( fnq [...] )` binds NAME to the fnq
# value. The paren wrap is required in this spec subset because the
# spec engine doesn't yet defer-and-resume forward arg evaluation
# across function-word boundaries the way the full Go engine does
# via insertForward; pre-evaluating `fnq [...]` inside a paren gets
# the FnDef onto the stack as a literal before `defq` consumes its
# body slot.
# Once defined, occurrences of NAME dispatch through the fnq:
# args fill the params in source order, the body runs in a subq-
# engine with each param bound on the defq stack, and the body's
# result splices back into the caller stack.
# tokens
# Single Integer param.
defq double ( fnq [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a a addq ] ) 5 double
defq inc ( fnq [ n:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ n 1 addq ] ) 7 inc
defq square ( fnq [ x:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ x x mulq ] ) 6 square
# Two Integer params (forward + stack split).
defq addAB ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a b addq ] ) 3 addAB 4
defq subAB ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ subq a b ] ) 10 subAB 3
column3: a=3 (fwd), b=10 (stk); body `subq a b` → `subq 3 10` → sig=[3,10] → handler returns 10-3 = 7

column1: # Three Integer params, all-stack call form.
defq trip3 ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer c:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a b addq c addq ] ) 1 2 3 trip3
# Composition: one fnq calling another by name from inside its body.
defq double ( fnq [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a a addq ] ) defq quad ( fnq [ x:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ x double double ] ) 4 quad
# Parens isolate calls so each invocation binds fresh params.
defq double ( fnq [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ a a addq ] ) ( 3 double ) ( 5 double )
column3: without the parens the inner addq would greedily consume the second arg
